title: scleria biflora subsp. ferruginea
intro: Scleria biflora subsp. ferruginea, also known as ferrugineous sword sedge, is a species of flowering plant in the family Cyperaceae, commonly found in tropical regions. This plant is rich in medicinal properties, particularly its rhizomes, which are used to treat various health conditions due to their antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Scleria biflora subsp. ferruginea include its potential to treat fever, skin infections, and respiratory issues, as well as its use as a natural remedy for wounds and skin irritations. In traditional medicine, the rhizomes of this plant are often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and may also be used in the form of a paste or powder for topical application.
